Lack of economy; waste (of resources, etc.).
(chiefly British, derogatory, informal) A minor official or worker who refuses to be flexible in the application of rules to help a client or customer.
(logistics, transport) The final stage of delivery of goods, etc., from a distribution centre to the consumer, often involving greater effort or expense.

(transitive) To be or become aware of (something); to perceive. To acknowledge the existence of (something); to recognize. To take hold of (something) with understanding; to conceive (something) in the mind; to become cognizant of; to understand. To have a conception of (something); to consider, to regard. To anticipate (something, usually unpleasant); especially, to anticipate (something) with anxiety, dread, or fear; to dread, to fear. (archaic or obsolete, also figuratively) To seize or take (something); to take hold of. (law enforcement) To seize or take (a person) by legal process; to arrest. (obsolete) To feel (something) emotionally. To learn (something). (also figuratively) To take possession of (something); to seize.
The study of beer and beer-brewing.
(uncountable) The state when something (for example, a custom or a law) is no longer observed nor practised; disuse, obsolescence; (countable) an instance of this.
(transitive) To achieve or obtain (something) by complicated or deceitful methods; to finagle, to wangle. To cheat or deceive (someone).
(religion) An offering or sacrifice to a deity that is completely burned to ashes.
(countable) An invasive malignant tumour derived from epithelial tissue that tends to metastasize to other areas of the body.
(informal, dated or humorous) An exceptionally good-looking and sexually attractive person, particularly a man.

(intransitive, chiefly US, informal) To become aggressive and erratic, especially due to stress; specifically, to carry out a shooting spree at a workplace environment; also (more generally) to become very angry; to lose one's temper.

(chiefly historical) (countable, alchemy, also figuratively) A pure quality with the power to cure or to turn one substance into another; also, a substance such as a philosopher's stone able to turn one substance into another. (obsolete) The product of such a transformation. (countable, chemistry) A fine substance deposited by precipitation, formerly applied to certain white precipitates from metallic solutions. (obsolete) A concentrated extract of a substance.
Deserving derision (“treatment with disdain or contempt”).
An inhabitant of Earth, as opposed to one of heaven.
(obsolete) Chiefly in court of piepowders, etc. (sense 2): a traveller, particularly one on foot; a wayfarer; specifically, a travelling merchant.
A collection of wind turbines, especially a large-scale array, used to generate electricity.
Of a page in a book or other publication: having its corner folded down, either due to having been read many times, or intentionally as a sort of bookmark.
A large and violent whirlpool.

(transitive) To reduce (something) in quality or quantity (as effect, scope, speed, etc.) To change (one's career or lifestyle) to one which is not as well paid but less stressful and more personally rewarding. (automotive, cycling) To shift (a car or bicycle) into a lower gear.
(transitive, originally US military slang, informal, often humorous) To tell or force (someone) to volunteer to do something.

Any of various medium-large black seabirds of the family Phalacrocoracidae which dive into water for fish and other aquatic animals, found throughout the world except for islands in the centre of the Pacific Ocean; specifically, the great cormorant (Phalacrocorax carbo).
Lasting or remaining active throughout the year, or all the time.

(transitive) To create (a small ornamental handicraft also called a scrimshaw) by carving or engraving on bone (originally whalebone or whales' teeth), ivory, or other materials. To carve or engrave (bone, ivory, or other materials) with ornamental designs. To carve or engrave (ornamental designs) on bone, ivory, or other materials.
Relating to expenditure or expense, especially on luxury goods.
(nautical) Sea spray (clouds of water droplets) blown from the tops of waves by the wind and whipped along the surface of the sea.
Of or relating to the reign of Queen Victoria from 1837 to 1901, or that period.
Of a person: emaciated, wasted, weakened; of the body or part of it: atrophied, shrunken, withered.

(historical) A place reserved for people with infectious diseases (especially leprosy or plague) to live on a long-term basis. (also figuratively) A building such as a hospital, or occasionally a ship, used to temporarily isolate sick people to prevent the spread of infectious diseases; a quarantine.
To make (someone) uncomfortable; to discomfort, to disturb, to trouble.

(architecture) An intermediate floor or storey in between the main floors of a building; specifically, one that is directly above the ground floor which does not extend over the whole floorspace of the building, and so resembles a large balcony overlooking the ground floor; an entresol. (by extension) An apartment, room, etc., on such an intermediate floor. (Canada, US) The lowest balcony in an auditorium, cinema, theatre, etc.; the dress circle. (obsolete) Additional flooring laid over a floor to bring it up to some height or level. In full mezzanine window: a small window at the height of a mezzanine floor (sense 1.1) or an attic, used to light these floors.

A supply (especially a large one) of something kept for future use, specifically in case the cost of the item increases or if there a shortage. (specifically, military, weaponry) A supply of nuclear weapons kept by a country.
(uncountable) The carrying or transportation of goods by a porter (“person who carries luggage and related objects”) or other person.
To a high degree of precision; with the utmost exactness; to a nicety.

Steadfastly faithful or loyal; unwavering in loyalty; staunch, true. (specifically) Patriotic. (British) Of or pertaining to the (historical) Tory, and now the Conservative, political party; hence, steadfastly conservative. (Scotland, historical) Of or pertaining to the Scottish Presbyterian or Whig political party in the 17th century; hence, steadfastly Presbyterian.
(chiefly Malta) A small, usually roundish lump of cheese; a baby cheese; specifically, a particular type made with sheep's milk in Malta.
Relating to Ukraine or its people.
Of, relating to, or being the person or entity after which someone or something is named; serving as an eponym.
(uncountable) Devilish action or conduct; wickedness; (more generally) troublemaking behaviour; mischief; (countable) an instance of this.
(biochemistry, ecology) Any substance produced by an individual of one species (often an insect) that benefits the recipient which is of a different species but is harmful to the producer.
An effigy, typically made of straw and dressed in old clothes, fixed to a pole in a field to deter birds from eating crops or seeds planted there.
Distant or remote in relationship, space, or time.
Of or having the qualities of a sybarite (“a person devoted to luxury and pleasure”); dedicated to excessive comfort and enjoyment; decadent, hedonistic, self-indulgent.
Habitual laziness or sloth.

(mechanical engineering, rail transport) A pipe forming part of the exhaust system of a steam locomotive that discharges exhaust steam from the cylinders up into the smokebox beneath the chimney in order to increase the draught through the firebox; this speeds up the release of smoke through the chimney.
A rare species or type of bird.
Extremely offensive or unpleasant; very annoying, contemptible, or odious.
Of or pertaining to Jesus Christ as Lord.
A physical or emotional confrontation; a battle, a fight; also, a bitter rivalry.
(originally African-American Vernacular, slang) A younger (often female) love interest.
(also figuratively) Armed or prepared for battle. Of a place: occupied or surrounded by armed troops.
(dated) A female founder (“one who founds or establishes”).

Senses with an unfavourable connotation. Of a person or entity: generally or widely known for something negative; infamous. Of an act, situation, etc.: blameworthy in an obvious and offensive way; blatant, flagrant.
(nautical, rail transport, road transport, chiefly historical) A man responsible for the care and maintenance of a length of canal, railway, or road.
A forecast of the future course or outcome of a situation based on what is presently known; a prediction.
(derogatory) One who is prone to butt in, interrupt, or get involved where they are not welcome; a busybody.
(intransitive, originally US) Synonym of peter (“originally (mining), of a vein of ore: to be depleted of ore; now (generally), to diminish to nothing; to dwindle, to trail off”)

(American football) The position at the end of the offensive line whose primary jobs are to block and serve as a short receiver. A player (called an end) playing the position of tight end (sense 1.1).
(Irish politics) The head of the government of Ireland, comparable to a prime minister of a Commonwealth of Nations country.
(transitive) To say (words, etc.) indistinctly; to mutter.
(astronomy) The star visible to the naked eye which was in the past, is now, or will be in the future nearest a celestial pole of a planet.
One who uses words skilfully.
(entomology) Of an insect: that bores (“makes holes”); specifically, belonging to the Terebrantia suborder of thrips which bore using their ovipositors.
Of or relating to the Passeriformes order of perching birds, which are generally anisodactyl (“having three toes pointing forward and one back, which facilitates perching”).
(originally US, water sports) A board on which a person stands to ride for leisure which is pulled on a water surface by a motorboat.

(countable, uncountable) A type of pasta made in the shape of long thin strings. (by extension, countable, uncountable) A dish that has spaghetti (sense 1) as a main part of it, such as spaghetti bolognese.
(economics) An economic condition in which a small number of sellers exert control over the market of a commodity.
(philosophy) Of or relating to cognition or knowledge, its scope, or how it is acquired.
So precious as not to be obtainable or sold at any price; invaluable.
An imaginary rural county in the West Country of England.
(chemistry) A test involving the reaction of an acid with another chemical.
(criminal law, international law) A punishable offence under international law for violations of the laws of war by any person or persons, military or civilian.

(countable) A toy that rights itself when pushed over. (informal) A short, plump person (especially a child). (gymnastics) A forward roll or sideways roll. (Australia) Synonym of tumbleweed (“any plant which habitually breaks away from its roots once dry, forming a light, rolling mass which is driven by the wind from place to place”); specifically, the prickly Russian thistle (Kali tragus or Salsola tragus). (British, also attributively) A baked or steamed pudding made from suet pastry which is spread with fruit or jam (or occasionally other fillings) and then rolled up. (Canada, US) In full roly-poly bug: a small terrestrial invertebrate which tends to roll into a ball when disturbed, such as a woodlouse (suborder Oniscidea, especially a pill bug (family Armadillidiidae) or a sowbug (family Porcellionidae)) or a pill millipede (superorder Oniscomorpha). (obsolete) A mischievous or worthless person; a scoundrel, a rascal.
Synonym of heart (“the seat of the affections or love”).
(obsolete) To make (a person, an animal, etc.) a captive; to take prisoner; to capture, to subdue. (figuratively) To capture or control (the mind, etc.); to subdue, to subjugate.
A conventual or monastic community; also, a convent or monastery.
(British, law enforcement, informal) A police car; specifically (historical), one with a broad white stripe painted around its middle on a darker-coloured or, originally, black background.
Synonym of sighthound (“a hound that primarily hunts by sight and speed, instead of by scent and endurance”)
Resembling marble or a marble statue; cold, smooth, white, etc.; marblelike.
(obsolete) Protruding or sticking out, especially in a way that obstructs.

(Soviet Union, historical, slang) One of the citizens of the former Soviet Union who was refused permission to emigrate (typically but not exclusively a Jewish citizen denied permission to emigrate to Israel).

(uncountable) A substance made by bees (clade Anthophila) primarily from beeswax which has hexagonal cells to hold their larvae, and for storing pollen and honey to feed the larvae and themselves when other food is scarce; it is also eaten by humans as part of comb-honey; (countable) a single sheet made up of two layers of this substance.
A trichroic violet-blue variety of the mineral zoisite mined in Tanzania, used as a gemstone.

(originally Christianity) A festival or holiday commemorating the birth of Jesus Christ and incorporating various Christian, pre-Christian, pagan, and secular customs, which in Western Christianity is celebrated on December 25 (Christmas Day) in most places.
Moving upward; ascending, rising.
The previous experiences and life of a person, specifically (narratology, especially in film, television) a character in a dramatic work.
(fantasy, mythology) Small humanoid creatures, such as gnomes and halflings.
(telegraphy, historical) Synonym of radiotelegram (“a message, like a telegram, transmitted by wireless telegraphy (“telegraphy by radio rather than by transmission cables”)”).

(uncountable) The study of the way sounds function in languages, including accent, intonation, phonemes, stress, and syllable structure, and which sounds are distinctive units within a language; (countable) the way sounds function within a given language; a phonological system.
(obsolete) A concealed hiding place, especially of an animal; a burrow, hole, or lair.

(transitive) To send out (heat, light, or some other form of radiation) in the form of rays; to radiate. (often literary or poetic) To make (someone or something) bright by shining light on them or it; to brighten, to illuminate. (technology) To apply radiation other than visible light to (someone or something). To treat (food) with ionizing radiation to destroy pathogens. (medicine) To treat (a patient, or a cancerous growth or tumour) with radiation. (figurative, often literary or poetic) To animate or enliven (one's mood, or soul or spirit). To cause (one's face) to look beautiful, happy, or lively; to light up. To decorate (a place) splendidly. To enlighten (someone, their mind, etc.) intellectually or spiritually; to illuminate, to shed light on. To send out (something) as if in the form of rays; to diffuse, to radiate, to shed. (obsolete, figurative) To influence (something) as if with rays of heat, light, etc.
A year in the Julian or Gregorian calendar with an intercalary day added to February (in the Gregorian calendar, February 29), used to adjust for the extra hours of the solar year; a 366-day year.
(obsolete) To make (something) lucid (“bright, luminous; also, clear, transparent”).
(dentistry, teratology) The congenital absence of some or all primary or permanent teeth, caused by a rare genetic disorder.
(Australia, New Zealand) A person who holds (leases or owns) a run (“rural landholding for farming”), especially one for raising sheep.
A small ornament of minor value; a knick-knack, a trinket.
(archaic or historical) A person who writes for a gazette or newspaper; a journalist; (specifically) a journalist engaged by a government.
(hunting, rare) Of or relating to hunting (especially with dogs).

(religion) Originally, the killing (and often burning) of a human being or an animal as an offering to a deity; later, also the offering of an object to a deity. A human being or an animal, or a physical object or immaterial thing (see sense 1.3), offered to a deity. (figurative) The offering of devotion, penitence, prayer, thanksgiving, etc., to a deity. (Christianity, specifically) Jesus Christ's voluntary offering of himself to God the Father to be crucified as atonement for the sins of humankind. (by extension) The rite of Holy Communion or the Mass, regarded as (Protestantism) an offering of thanksgiving to God for Christ's crucifixion, or (Roman Catholicism) a perpetual re-enactment of Christ's sacrificial offering.
(countable) A scale insect of the species Dactylopius coccus, native to the tropical and subtropical Americas, which lives on prickly pear cacti (genus Opuntia).
(archaic) Having the temperament of a termagant (noun sense 1); bad-tempered, brawling, boisterous, turbulent.
A type of urban explorer who visits the ancient catacombs and quarries linked by tunnels beneath Paris, France.
On this side of the Alps (chiefly the south side where Rome is located).
Done with intensity or to a great degree; thorough.

(firearms, historical) A large, portable firearm normally supported by wheels, in which bullets may be fired from a row of several tubes in succession; it was chiefly used from the 14th to the 17th century.

(uncountable) Often followed by against, between, for, or to: a (deep) feeling of dislike or repugnance, normally towards a person and less often towards a thing, often without any conscious reasoning; aversion, distaste, hostility; (countable) an instance of this.
Any magic trick performed with a hat, especially one involving pulling an object (traditionally a rabbit) out of an apparently empty hat.
(uncountable, also figurative) Increase by natural growth, especially the gradual increase of organic bodies by the internal addition of matter; organic growth; also, the amount of such growth.
Capable of forming something. (biology) Capable of producing new tissue. (linguistic morphology) Pertaining to the formation of words; specifically, of an affix: forming words through inflection.
(originally and chiefly US, informal) Lacking in interest or substance; boring, disappointing, lacklustre.

(countable) A failure, particularly one which is mechanical in nature. (physics, uncountable) The sudden (and usually damaging) transition of an electrical insulator to a conductor when subjected to a sufficiently strong voltage, caused by the partial or complete ionization of the insulator; (countable) an instance of this; also, the minimum voltage at which this occurs.

(linguistics, prosody) A separation of one syllable (especially a vowel which is a diphthong, that is, beginning with one sound and ending with another) into two distinct syllables; distraction. (orthography) The diacritical mark consisting of two dots (¨) placed over a letter (especially the second of two consecutive vowels) to indicate that it is sounded separately, usually as a distinct syllable. An occurrence of separate vowel sounds in adjacent syllables without an intervening consonant; a hiatus.
Used with the adverb sense of about. (transitive, archaic except chiefly Northern England) To circulate or spread (news, a rumour, etc.)
(equestrianism) A rein of a horse held loosely so that the animal can move more freely; also, the free condition that this gives to a horse.

(transitive) (archaic, rare) Synonym of liquefy (“to make (something) into a liquid”); to liquidize. (figurative) To make (a sound) less harsh. To use up (money or other assets) wastefully; to dissipate, to squander, to waste. (informal) To kill (someone), usually violently, and especially for some ideological or political aim; to assassinate, to murder; also, to abolish or eliminate (something); to do away with, to put an end to. (business, commercial law, finance) To convert (assets) into cash; to encash, to realize, to redeem. To settle (a debt) by paying the outstanding amount; to pay off. To settle the financial affairs of (a corporation, partnership, or other business) with the aim of ceasing operations, by determining liabilities, using assets to pay debts, and apportioning the remaining assets if any; to wind up. (obsolete) To make (something) clear and intelligible. To resolve or settle (differences, disputes, etc.). (chiefly law) To ascertain (an amount of money), especially by agreement or through litigation; also, to set out (financial accounts) properly.

Chiefly followed by on or upon: leaning, or lying, reclining, or resting, on something else. (botany) Of an anther: lying on the inner side of the filament; also, of a cotyledon: having its back lying against the radicle. (zoology) Of a body part such as a hair, spine, or wing: bent downwards or otherwise positioned so that it, or part of it, rests on or touches something else; specifically (ornithology), of the hind toe of a bird: fully resting on a support.
(informal) Adorable in a dorky, socially awkward manner.
(chiefly historical) Situated beneath the moon; specifically, between the moon and the earth.

(Cockney rhyming slang, originally military slang) Synonym of tea (“(uncountable) a drink made by infusing dried leaves or buds of the tea plant (Camellia sinensis) in hot water; (countable) a cup of this drink”)
(biology) Synonym of serotine (“developing at a later time or later in a season, especially than is customary with allied species; specifically (botany), of a plant: flowering late in a season”).
(obsolete, rare) One who guides strangers; a guide or tour guide.
(transitive, electronics, slang, uncommon) To destroy (a defective electronic component) using a hammer.

(arithmetic) The arithmetic operator consisting of repeated exponentiation, by analogy with exponentiation being repeated multiplication and multiplication being repeated addition, b a {\displaystyle ^{b}a} denoting a {\displaystyle a} to the power of a {\displaystyle a} to the power of … {\displaystyle \ldots } to the power of a {\displaystyle a} , in which a {\displaystyle a} appears b {\displaystyle b} times.
Characteristic of or resembling Albert Einstein, or his theory of relativity; Einsteinian. Highly intelligent; brilliant. Of hair: sticking out wildly.
Sometimes in full entrecôte steak: a premium cut of beef from between the ribs used for roasts and steaks.
One million deaths, especially as a unit of measure for estimating deaths due to nuclear warfare.
(transitive, medicine) Synonym of cannulate (“to insert a cannula (“tube to drain or inject fluid”) into (a body part such as a cavity, organ, or vessel (especially a vein))”).

A peninsula in Turkey, north of the Dardanelles, between the Aegean and Marmara seas. (by ellipsis, historical) The Gallipoli Campaign (1915–1916) in World War I. (dated) Synonym of Gelibolu: a town and municipality of Turkey located on the Gallipoli peninsula (sense 1).

(transitive) To perceive (something) visually; to see. To depict (something) in a way which can be seen. To form a mental picture of (something); to picture (something) in the mind; to envisage. (chiefly medicine) To make (a hidden or unclear body part, process, or object) visible by optical methods (such as endoscopy, magnetic resonance imaging, ultrasound, or X-rays), or other techniques.
A partitioned, traditionally lacquered, lunchbox in which a bento (“Japanese takeaway lunch”) is served.
(informal) A form of speaking in which word order is altered.
Not rebuilt. Of something damaged or destroyed: not built again, not reconstructed. Of something existing: not modified or renovated; still in its original form.

(Judaism) Synonym of Shavuot (“a Jewish harvest festival which falls on the sixth day of Sivan in the spring, fifty days after the second day of the Passover when the omer (“sheaf of barley”) is offered; a ceremony held on that day to commemorate the giving of the Torah (“first five books of the Hebrew scriptures”) to Moses and the Israelites on Mount Sinai”).

(martial arts, wrestling) A grappling hold around a person's neck, especially one in which the neck is grasped tightly from behind with an arm, cutting off the flow of blood to the brain and restricting breathing.
(biblical, mythology) A vast sea monster of tremendous strength, either imaginary or real, described as the most dangerous and powerful creature in the ocean.
(entomology, loosely, also attributive) A mosquito of the genus Anopheles, some insects of which transmit various parasites of the genus Plasmodium that are the cause of malaria.
(intransitive, slang) To lie quiet and still in order to avoid detection; also (by extension), to stay hidden by being discreet and not drawing attention; to keep a low profile, to lie low.
To forcibly impose obedience, servitude, or submission upon (a country, a people, etc.).

(transitive) To paint (a building, a wall, etc.) a bright white with whitewash (noun sense 1). (figurative) (derogatory) To distort or give a misleading account of (history, a narrative, etc.) by discounting the participation of people of colour and focusing on white people. (chiefly film, television) To cast a white actor in a role for a person of colour in (a film, television programme, etc.). (media) To make over (someone of colour) so that they appear more white, for example, by applying lightening makeup to their skin or digitally manipulating an image of them. (chiefly literary and poetic, often passive voice) To cover or coat (something) with a white substance, as if with whitewash (noun sense 1); also, to light up (something) with white light. (business) To clear (someone's) debts, especially through a declaration of bankruptcy by a court; also, to clear or write off (a debt). (often politics) To hide (someone's) mistakes, or to create an appearance of honesty, propriety, etc., for (someone); also, to intentionally hide or overlook (mistakes, unfavourable facts, wrongdoing, etc.). (sports, originally baseball, informal) To achieve a complete victory or series of victories over (an opponent) without suffering any losses; also, to achieve a victory or series of victories over (an opponent) by a very large margin. (cosmetics, archaic or historical) To make (the face, etc.) look lighter with makeup or a similar preparation. (obsolete) Synonym of bleach (“to treat (fabric) with a substance which lightens or whitens”).
(military) An artillery soldier who maintains and operates (historical) a cannon, or (now) some other piece of heavy artillery.

(transitive) (graphic design, photography) To inadvertently or intentionally reduce the number of colours in (a photograph or other image), changing a continuous gradation of tone to several regions of fewer tones with abrupt changes from one tone to another. (Canada, US, basketball, slang) To score a slam dunk in basketball against (one or more opposing players) by leaping over them; also (by extension, sports, slang), to score a goal, a point, etc., in some other sport against (one or more opposing players) in a spectacular manner.
Of a place: not having tracks or paths; pathless, untrodden; also, having had all tracks removed.

(numismatics, historical, also attributive) Senses chiefly with the plural form halfpennies. (UK) A discontinued English (later British) coin worth half of one penny (old or new). (by extension, Ireland, Scotland) A similar coin formerly used in Ireland and Scotland. Senses chiefly with the plural form halfpence. (UK) An amount of money that the English (later British) coin was worth. (by extension, Ireland, Scotland) An amount of money that the Irish or Scottish coin was worth.
(figurative) Completely, wholly. (archaic) Of acts or speech: directly and unambiguously; clearly, plainly.

A grid of horizontal and vertical lines; specifically, one used as a guide to proportionately enlarge or reduce a drawing. (geography) The network of lines of latitude and longitude that make up a coordinate system such as the one used for charts and maps of the Earth. (UK, optics) Synonym of reticle (“a transparent plate marked with a crosshair, grid, or scale which is used in an optical instrument, etc., to aid in alignment, counting, or measurement of objects being observed; also, the crosshair, grid, or scale on such a plate”).

(figurative) To embellish or vary (something) by interspersing or mixing another thing with it; also, to intersperse or mix (something) into another thing to embellish or vary it; to interweave, to mingle.
(systems engineering) The science of risk analysis.
(transitive) To cancel or withdraw an invitation to (someone).
Not having feathers. Of a bird: not yet having developed its feathers and wings and become able to fly.

(countable) The outward appearance or form of a person or thing. Followed by of: the outward appearance of a person or thing when regarded as similar to that of another person or thing. Followed by of: the outward appearance of a person or thing which is different from what the person or thing actually is; also, an outward appearance of a thing which does not actually exist.
Of or relating to hubris (“excessive arrogance or pride, or presumption”); hence, overly arrogant or proud, or presumptuous, especially in a disdainful or rude manner.

(chiefly South Asia, especially India) Synonym of fluorescent lamp (“a gas-discharge lamp that uses fluorescence to produce visible light, especially one consisting of a tube filled with mercury vapour which emits ultraviolet radiation when an electric current is passed through it, causing fluorescence when the radiation hits a phosphor coating on the interior of the tube”); also, a light fixture containing one or more fluorescent lamps.
(originally and chiefly US, informal) Of or relating to New Yorkers of Puerto Rican descent.
(fiction, history) In fictional or historical writings about chivalric romances: a quest embarked upon by a knight or other person which is not the main quest.
(uncountable, also attributive) The light reflected from the Moon, which seems to emanate from it.

(transitive) To cut up or dissect (the body of a human being or an animal), specifically for the purpose of investigating its anatomy. To punish (someone) by post mortem dissection following execution. To cut up or dissect (a plant or one of its parts) to investigate its structure. (figurative) To scrutinize (something) down to the most minute detail. (obsolete) To chemically analyse (a substance).
(music) Of or relating to, or characteristic of, Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart or his music.

(evolutionary theory, uncountable) Charles Darwin's theory regarding the evolution of living organisms through natural selection (set out chiefly in his works On the Origin of Species, 1859; and The Descent of Man, and Selection in Relation to Sex, 1871); also, belief in this theory. (uncountable) Short for neo-Darwinism (“the synthesis of Charles Darwin's theory of evolution through natural selection with the modern genetic understanding of heredity”). (by extension) Chiefly preceded by a descriptive word. (biology, uncountable) Any of various theories in biology which apply aspects of Darwin's theory (noun sense 1) such as adaptation, competition, or gradual evolution; also, belief in such a theory. (pseudoscience, uncountable) Any of various theories, now generally discredited, which apply aspects of Darwin's theory (noun sense 1) to other situations such as the development of ideas, organizations, or social groups. (generally, countable, uncountable) A process of gradual evolution; also, ruthless competition for achievement or survival.
Of a radio wave: having a wavelength of between 10 and 100 metres, corresponding to a frequency between 3 and 30 megahertz.
(uncountable) Synonym of precariousness (“the state of being uncertain or unstable”); (countable) an instance of this.
A wolf that is not part of a pack.
(archaic, rare) A female person who executes or carries out something.

(dated) Now chiefly home economics: the art or science of household management, especially relating to the appropriate organization of resources; housekeeping. Management of household finances; also, the financial situation of a household.

(transitive) To convey (information, a message, news, etc.) using a telephone (noun sense 1). To (attempt to) contact (someone) using a telephone. (obsolete) To provide (a place) with a telephone system. To transmit (sounds) over a distance.
A referential book such as a handbook or manual; A useful object, constantly carried on one's person .
a detailed graphic representation of the surface features of a place or object; the features themselves (the terrain); the surveying of the features
Uncontrollable sexual desire, found in a man (contrast to nymphomania)
A platform used to display or convey a coffin during a funeral, often ornate
One employed to take dictation or copy manuscripts; a clerk, secretary or stenographer
An uproar or fuss
Of fourth rank or order
A percussive exercise which involves playing a series of hits in the pattern RLRRLRLL
A rapid dance in 6 / 8 {\displaystyle 6/8} time, originating in Italy; the dance was believed to cure poisonous spider bites
Of, or relating to right angles; perpendicular to.
Having lustful desires; characterized by lewdness; sensual; lascivious
Not bound by chains or shackles.
A waste of time and/or money; a pointless activity
Resembling tufts of wool.
A lover of combs
An indirect or deceptive device or strategem; a blind.
To skim the surface of a body of water while moving at high speed
Being everywhere at once; omnipresent.
A phrase in which the initial (usually consonantal) sounds of two or more of the main words are accidentally transposed
A form of error arising from mishearing a spoken or sung phrase
A dirty, shabbily-clothed child; an urchin
To associate with others in a friendly manner, especially with an enemy
Promoting health or well-being; wholesome
Tearful; causing tears or crying
Inducing torpor; tending to cause apathy or lethargy
To make better, to improve; to heal or solve a problem
A form of metaphor, whereby an inclusive term stands for something included, or vice versa
Anything that indicates future trends
One who sells by deception; a con artist; a charlatan
One who opposes orthodoxy and religion.
To shine light on something.
Random; determined randomly
To assume crystalline form.
Behaving as though affected by alcohol
Excessively particular, demanding, or fussy about details.
Gloomy, mournful or dismal, especially to an exaggerated degree
(From Ancient Greek οἱ πολλοί): The common people; the masses
(From Hindustani जगन्नाथ): A literal or metaphorical force regarded as unstoppable
A period or occasion of general license; a period of unrestrained revelry
Talkative, chatty; given to excess conversation
A person who makes a hobby of photography
Not direct or to the point.
(rhetoric) An exclamatory piece of dialogue addressed to someone or something, especially absent
To make hurtful untrue comments about someone; to slander
Part of or similar to rhetoric; pertaining to the use of language as a means to persuade
(of a voice) Loud, powerful, booming; suitable for giving speeches to large crowds
To a degree that is negligible; so small or slight that it may be ignored
Seemingly, apparently, reputedly
To end all resistance; to give up or surrender
Government by the wealthy.
A member of a supposed prehistoric race that lived in caves or holes.
An ill-tempered person full of stubborn ideas or opinions
In disrepair or disorder; poorly maintained
To bring oneself into favour, especially by flattery or trying to please
Extremely angry or furious, to the point of being unable to move or speak
Spacious and convenient; roomy and comfortable.
A statement or idea which is unproven, but is thought to be true; a guess.
A writing desk with a hinged door that provides the writing surface
Not easily excited to action or passion; calm; sluggish.
Sinuous; curving in alternate directions.
Of a fine, almost transparent texture.
To laugh loudly, immoderately, or too often
Characterized by exceptionally early development or maturity.
Mob rule; government by the masses
A child prodigy; a phenom
Of doubtful authenticity.
(archaic) To drive away; to overcome
A name for the hash or pound sign (#), used mainly in telephony and computing
A manuscript or document that has been erased or scraped clean, for reuse of the paper, parchment, vellum, or other medium on which it was written
Glorification, sometimes to a divine level; deification; crediting a person with god-like power.
Not belonging to, or dependent upon, a thing; not essential or intrinsic; foreign
In a manner that is impossible to avoid or prevent; as expected.
Drunkenly; as if intoxicated
(idiomatic) Vaguely, without clear purpose or specific intention.
(idiomatic) Very hot; originally from a literary comparison in The Canterbury Tales
Meekly; self-consciously; with embarrassment
With jubilation or triumph.
A mirrored sphere designed to slowly rotate while suspended from the ceiling
Having a bad smell
(linguistics) The insertion of a phoneme, letter, or syllable into a word, usually to satisfy the phonological constraints of a language or poetic context
To pronounce with an accent or vocal stress.
Able to take hold of and clasp objects; adapted for grasping especially by wrapping around an object
(linguistics) Having multiple meanings or interpretations
The action of kissing.
Of, pertaining to, or resembling a hedgehog
To obscure something by casting a shadow.
A musical instrument endemic to Australia, consisting of a long hollowed out log, which produces a low, deep drone when blown into
(dialectal) A bumblebee
Simultaneously feeling opposing or contradictory feelings.
A prank in which a gullible victim is sent off on a fruitless search for a nonexistent item
A long body-length pillow that can be held or wrapped around one's body while sleeping.
(of a married couple) Living with the family of the wife
Good-natured banter; raillery.
Equivalent in meaning or effect
(US) A dense baked or fried flatbread made of cornmeal
Streaked, spotted, or otherwise marked with a variety of color
A tightly-compressed bandage used to stop bleeding by stopping the flow of blood through a large artery in a limb
An abundance or plentiful supply.
Fawning or subservient.
Ambiguous or meaningless language.
The process of extracting fragrance from flowers by using unscented fats to capture the essential oils
A competitive eater
In a shape reminding of an ellipse; oval.
To discourage someone by removing their enthusiasm or courage
An electrically insulating or nonconducting material
To incorporate nutrients into the body after digestion.
Someone who puts an end to harmless fun
(informal) An impromptu fight with the fists, usually between only two people.
Obstinate and unruly; strongly opposed to something.
To bring to completion; to accomplish.
Describing one who enjoys being in crowds and socializing.
A food consisting of chopped meat and pickled herring, with oil, vinegar, pepper, and onions.
(art) The distinctive pattern of hairline cracks in the surface of an old painting
Toothless
An official license to publish or print something, especially when censorship applies.
Generated or caused by light.
A person from ancient Philistia.
(informal) A fit of anger or panic; conniption fit.
(archaic) gentleness, meekness
To fall into ruin or disuse.
(obsolete) Delay, hesitation, procrastination
Oily, greasy.
Advocating a policy of revenge or retaliation
A skin discoloration caused by bleeding underneath the skin; a bruise.
Casually calm and relaxed.
Capable of causing fire.
(biology) An outer protective covering such as the feathers or skin of an animal, a rind or shell.
Very small.
General, universal, worldwide.
Resembling an overbearing woman; shrew-like.
To put into obligatory isolation or separation, especially as a sanitary measure to prevent the spread of contagious disease
(nautical) The third mast from the bow on a ship having three or more masts.
A stylistic scheme in which words from the same root are used together, or a word is repeated in a different inflection or case
(architecture) A row of balusters topped by a rail, serving as an open parapet, as along the edge of a balcony
Prone to relapse into immoral or antisocial behavior
An important, powerful or influential person.
Any of a person's direct ancestors; an individual from whom one or more people are descended.
A word, especially seen as a test, to distinguish someone as belonging to a particular nation, class, profession etc.
To act in the role of a person responsible for the highest level of planning and execution of a major operation
A person who lives and works on the land.
To examine something with great care.
(of a person) Not marked by promptness or decision.
Out of alignment, crooked, cater-corner.
(Northern England) Exclamation of surprise or pleasure
(in a modern theater) The stage area between the curtain and the orchestra.
Contemptibly modern, unfamiliar, or different
A convert to Christianity under instruction before baptism; a young Christian preparing for confirmation
Any of several short-lived herbs or shrubs of the genus Erysimum with bright yellow to red flowers.
A pit-like structure created by an impacting meteoroid, asteroid or comet
Undeniably important; describing that which cannot be played down or ignored.
Connected; touching; abutting.
Regardful of others; beneficent; unselfish

(historical, also figuratively) A machine used for the application of capital punishment by decapitation, consisting of a tall upright frame from which is suspended a heavy diagonal-edged blade which is dropped on to the neck of the person to be executed; also, execution using this machine.
(dance, dated) A card on which a person (usually a woman) lists those they have agreed to dance with.
Used to warn of consequences if a specified act is done or not done: subject to the punishment of (some penalty); or (generally) subject to the circumstance of (some thing).
Skill or technique involving the use of a camera, especially a video camera.
(dance, music) A kind of mosh pit (“spontaneous dance floor in front of a stage when people mosh”) in which the participants follow a circular path, leaving the centre clear.
Pertaining to democracy; constructed upon or in line with the principle of government chosen by the people.
(informal) Synonym of information overload (“the availability or supply of too much information, or a state of stress which results from it”)
Directed or moving backwards in relation to the normal or previous direction of travel; retreating.
(uncountable) Uncultivated and unsettled land in its natural state inhabited by wild animals and with vegetation growing wild; (countable) a tract of such land; a waste or wild.
Guilt for one's own actions.
(informal) Proficient in the use of technology, especially computers.

Either of two species of fast-running bird in the genus Geococcyx of the cuckoo family native to North and Central America, the lesser roadrunner (Geococcyx velox) and, especially, the greater roadrunner (Geococcyx californianus).
Of, pertaining to, or created by the English author Charles Dickens.
On a large scale; magnified.
(formal, chiefly archaic) Of or pertaining to breakfast; specifically, one taken early in the morning or immediately upon getting up.
Causing, or likely to cause, an uproar.
(art, literature) The presence of traces of a previous work in an artistic or literary work; especially (painting) an image which has been painted over but is still detectable.

Any of several small hornless ruminants of the family Tragulidae (genera Hyemoschus, Moschiola, and Tragulus), native to tropical rainforests of South and Southeast Asia, and Central and West Africa; a mouse deer.

(Canada, music) A traditional Newfoundland musical instrument fashioned from household and toolshed items, typically a mop handle attached with bottle caps, small bells, tin cans, etc., which are struck with a drumstick while one end of the handle is hit against the floor.
(uncountable) An Italian coffee-based beverage made from espresso to which milk that has been steamed and/or frothed is added; (countable) a cup of this beverage.
Not penitent; specifically (Christianity), not repenting of one's sins; unrepentant.
(countable, archaic) A cause, object, or situation of disgrace or shame.
(neologism) Contempt for, hatred of, or prejudice against black women.
Chiefly followed by by or with: to preoccupy (someone) in an emotional or mental way, so as to preclude other things.
The quality or state of being a lesbian.

(cycle racing) A cyclist on a cycle racing team whose role is to assist the team's designated leaders (for example, by riding in front of them to create a slipstream, or to set the pace), even if at the expense of their own individual performance.
(transitive) To criticize (someone or something) in an abusive or harsh manner. To attack (someone or something) with abusive language; to revile, to vilify.

(transitive) To make a humble request to (someone, especially a person in authority); to beg, to beseech, to entreat. (specifically, Oxford University, archaic) Of a member of the university, or an alumnus or alumna of another university seeking a degree ad eundem: to make a formal request (to the university) that an academic degree be awarded to oneself. (specifically, religion) To make a humble request to (a deity or other spiritual being) in a prayer; to entreat as a supplicant. To ask or request (something) humbly and sincerely, especially from a person in authority; to beg or entreat for.
(idiomatic) To assume a humble tone. To not say anything; to keep quiet. To play a minor part.
(military, weaponry, archaic) Pertaining to a catapult or catapults.
The collective people of a country, state, or electoral district who are entitled to vote.
Not wavering. Always on course and steady. Not doubting or unsure; decisive, firm, resolute.
(literary) Bookselling.

(India) The practice of Gandhism (“the philosophy of Mahatma Gandhi, encompassing tenets such as non-violent activism”), often expressed through unorthodox forms of activism such as depositing flowers or planting trees.

(physiology) Of a stimulus: below the limen or threshold of conscious perception, especially if still able to produce a response; also (generally), below the threshold where a response can be produced.
(military, archaic except humorous) A man whose occupation is fighting in wars; a soldier, a warrior.
(chiefly US) The string or lace used to secure a shoe to the foot; a shoelace.
One of a series of small rectangular fortifications, spaced roughly one Roman mile apart, built during the period of the Roman Empire.

(law, chiefly historical) (uncountable) Criminal neglect or wrongful execution of duty, especially by a public official; (countable) a specific instance of this. (uncountable) In full misprision of felony or misprision of treason: originally, a less serious form of felony or treason; later, the crime of (intentionally) failing to give information about a felony or treason that one knows about; (countable) an instance of this.
(historical) A woman's fur-lined cloak, mantle, or pelisse with large sleeves, worn during the early 19th century.
(slang) A chamber pot enclosed in a box; a portable commode.
People who fish for a living.

A reference work with a list of words from one or more languages, normally ordered alphabetically, explaining each word's meanings (senses), and sometimes also containing information on its etymology, pronunciation, usage, semantic relations, and translations, as well as other data.
(physiology, historical) A sperm cell or spermatozoon; also, the embryo that was formerly thought to be contained inside a spermatozoon in a fully developed state.

(Australia, New Zealand) An informal gossip network, especially in a rural area such as the outback; also, a component in the network (either a person or an imagined wire). A message or story transmitted through such an informal network, especially one containing false information.
A person belonging to two cultures.
(archaic except poetic) Perpetual, or made perpetual; continued for an indefinite time.

(rail transport) A railway track on a steep slope in a zigzag formation, in which a train travels in a reverse direction at each switch. A railway track on which there are steep ascents and descents, a train moving partially or fully by the force of gravity using the momentum generated when descending to travel up an ascending part of the track; especially (British, dated), such a track built for fun rides at an amusement park; a type of rollercoaster.
(uncountable, pulmonology, archaic) Breathing difficulty, shortness of breath.
(Christianity, neologism) The period of the COVID-19 pandemic.
A hit to the elbow; also, an injury to the elbow caused by such a hit.

(pathology, neologism) Long-term sequelae or symptoms (such as brain fog, extreme fatigue, or shortness of breath) following a COVID-19 infection, which persist after the SARS-CoV-2 virus is no longer active.
(chiefly Canada, US, idiomatic) A current prevailing situation which has emerged recently, that differs dramatically from the previous one and is expected to remain.

(Scotland, games, historical) An old Scottish parlour game in which people were chosen, usually by throwing dice, to perform some humorous act or drink a large amount of an alcoholic beverage, with a forfeit if they were unable to do so.
(psychology, physiology) The study of humour and laughter, and its effects on the body.
Originally, any frozen dew forming a white deposit on exposed surfaces.
Unsure how to act or respond; bewildered, perplexed.
(cooking, also attributively) Poultry which has been cut along the spine and spread out for more even cooking.

(transitive) To reduce the volume of (music, a sound, etc.). (figuratively) To reduce the force or impact of (something); to damp, to mute; especially, to minimize the less desirable aspects of (something); to play down, to tone down. (figuratively) To attempt to persuade someone about (something) through understatement, so that the listener accepts the good points as obvious.
(idiomatic) The very best that one can do.

(transitive) To destroy (someone or something) completely, leaving no trace; to annihilate, to wipe out. To hide (something) by covering it; to conceal, to obscure. (also figuratively) To make (a drawing, text which is printed or written, etc.) indecipherable, either by erasing or obscuring it; to blot out, to efface, to delete. (biology, pathology, surgery, chiefly passive voice) To impair the function and/or structure of (a body cavity, vessel, etc.) by ablating or occluding it (in the latter case, chiefly by filling it with tissue). (philately) To cancel (a postage stamp) with a postmark so it cannot be reused.
(also attributively) An act of investing with a crown; a crowning.
A trusted man whose job is to assist and organize someone else's day-to-day business or personal tasks; a personal assistant, a right-hand man; also, a trusted male companion.
(architecture, dated, also figuratively) A story of a building below the stories generally used for residence or work.
(British, Ireland, Christianity) Not designated as a parish (“an administrative part of a diocese that has its own church”).
To be deplored. To be felt sorrow for; worthy of compassion; lamentable. Deserving strong condemnation; shockingly bad, wretched.

(countable, botany, also figuratively) A plant with flowers which turn to face and follow the sun, such as (archaic) marigolds and sunflowers. (specifically) A plant of the genus Heliotropium, especially the common heliotrope (Heliotropium arborescens) which has clusters of purple flowers with a strong fragrance. With a qualifying word: any of various plants resembling those of the genus Heliotropium.

Not together. Not cohesive or united; disunited, disparate, separated; also, not coordinated; uncoordinated. (informal) Of a person or their behaviour: not composed; disorganized, sloppy. (informal) Of a situation: not under control; chaotic, disorganized.

Senses relating to an area of land with plants on it. A strip of land with plants in a place which is otherwise bare. A strip of land on which trees are planted to protect against desertification, soil erosion, etc. (urban studies) A strip of land next to a building, open space, road, etc., with plants on it, intended for exercise or recreation. (chiefly British) An area of agricultural land around an urban area that is protected from large-scale building.
Of poor quality; amateurish, shoddy, small-time.
(idiomatic) Synonym of tall tale (“a tale or story which is fantastic and greatly exaggerated; also, an account of questionable veracity; a lie, an untruth”)
(law, politics) A direct popular vote on an issue of public importance, such as an amendment to the constitution, a change in the sovereignty of the nation, or some government policy; a referendum.
Generous and tireless with one's contributions of money, time, etc.
Thousand-year-old; also (by extension, loosely) thousands of years old.

Synonym of cuttlefish bone (“the calcareous, oval-shaped internal shell of a cuttlefish (order Sepiida), originally used in powdered form as an antacid and for absorbing moisture or polishing objects, and now chiefly as a dietary supplement for cage birds”)
(transitive) To select (someone or something) from a group and highlight them or treat them differently.
(video games, also attributive) A gameworld that the player may traverse freely, rather than being restricted to certain predefined areas and quests.
Characterized by iniquity (“deviation from what is right”). Morally objectionable; sinful, wicked. Grossly unfair or unjust.
(nonstandard, often humorous) One who is assassinated.
Having no blemish or stain; absolutely clean and tidy.
(pathology) Of an infection or its cause: arising from the environment of, or treatment in, a hospital.
(idiomatic, intransitive, chiefly US) Usually followed by with: to associate closely; to consort, mingle, or socialize.
Of cloth, clothing, furnishings, etc.: frayed and worn to an extent that the nap is damaged and the warp and weft threads show; shabby, worn-out.
Formed or occurring beneath a glacier or other body of ice.
(dated) The domain of the kitchen or people who work there.
(also attributive) A drop-shaped diamond or other gem used as a pendant; also, a piece of jewellery in pendant form.
A friend who supports someone; a person who has someone's back; a backer, a supporter.
Of two or more objects: being in the same location.
A toy weapon consisting of a tube through which dried peas or small objects are blown.
A hairstyle worn by Rastafarians and others in which the hair is left to grow long, and twisted into matted strings.
Having no matter or substance; incorporeal.
A physical image or representation of a deity, person, or thing.
Cygnus atratus, a swan with black plumage and a red bill which is endemic to Australia.
(US politics, slang, humorous) The process by which candidates for the Presidency of the United States choose a running mate to become the Vice President if elected.

(Maine, mining, historical) A customized horse- or ox-drawn stoneboat or wagon once used for hauling stone from quarries, with smaller front wheels and larger rear ones, and a derrick to lift blocks of stone on and off the vehicle.

Also occasionally in the singular form plum rain: the East Asian rainy season from early summer to midsummer in the fourth and fifth lunar months (early June to early or mid-July); also, the rain which falls during this season.

Of or pertaining to Prometheus, a demigod in Greek mythology who created mortals from clay and stole fire from Zeus to give to them, for which Zeus punished him by chaining him to a rock and having an eagle feed on his liver which grew back each night; he was later rescued by Heracles. Boldly creative, inventive, or original; skilful, talented; also, recklessly daring; audacious. Of a Romantic literary hero: defying traditional moral categories; rebelling against a larger order; persecuted but dauntless.

Of or pertaining to, or having the characteristics of, a phantasm (“something seen but having no physical reality”); imaginary, unreal. Of or pertaining to, or having the characteristics of, a phantom (“apparition or ghost”); ghostly, spectral. (parapsychology) Of or pertaining to, or having the characteristics of, a phantasm (“perception or vision of a living or dead person who is not physically present, often through telepathy”).
Someone or something that is unbearable and so particularly avoided or disliked; the bane of someone's existence; an object of aversion; an anathema.
(informal) A creation of one's brain; an original idea or innovation of a person or group of people, an organization, etc.

A person of mixed European and African descent. An African person with European ancestors. (South Africa) Synonym of colored (“a person having ancestry from more than one of the racial groups of Southern Africa (black, white, and Asian)”).
(informal) Characterized or marked by wiggling.

Extraordinarily amazing. In a positive sense: marvellous, wonderful; (specifically) extremely talented, especially at a young age. In a negative sense: appalling, horrifying, shocking; (specifically, archaic) abnormal, freakish, monstrous, unnatural.
(archaic) Of or from a foreign country; not indigenous or native; alien, foreign.
Of a person: appearing at a surprisingly wide variety of historic events and/or with a diverse group of historic figures.
Lacking skill in physical movement with the hands; clumsy.
The length of time in one's life where one is generally in good health.
(linguistics) A secretive language form used by a subculture; an arcane cant.

(cooking, dated) A soft, muffin-sized, often heart-shaped cake, particularly popular in the 18th century, containing currants and flavoured with mace and sometimes lemons or oranges, which may be topped with chocolate or shredded coconut.
Of or pertaining to an alderman (“member of a municipal legislative body in a city or town”) or group of aldermen.

(countable) (often military) A place where people (such as displaced people, soldiers, or travellers) encamp, that is, stay in tents or other temporary structures; a camp or campsite. (figurative) A place where people or things stay temporarily. (by extension, archaeology) An enclosed or fortified prehistoric site, especially a European Iron Age hill-fort. (by extension, Freemasonry, obsolete) A meeting of Freemasons.

(idiomatic) The mental faculty or inner sense with which one produces or reproduces imagined or recalled sounds solely within the mind; the supposed organ within the mind which experiences such sounds.
Someone who has a fondness for teddy bears, usually a collector of them.
(nautical, chiefly historical) The manager or owner of a wharf (“artificial landing place for ships on a riverbank or shore”).
Synonym of ethics (“the study of principles relating to right and wrong conduct”).
(chiefly Ireland, Scotland, dated or archaic) Whiskey or whisky.
A child who is thirteen years old.

(intransitive) (obsolete) To come down or go down; to descend. (figurative) To come down from a superior position and do something; to deign; (with a negative connotation) to stoop. To treat someone as though inferior; to talk down to someone; to patronize. Chiefly followed by on or upon: to go into detail; to particularize, to specify. (obsolete) To agree to something; to accede, to assent, to consent; also, to reach an agreement. (obsolete) To give way or yield in a deferential manner; to be amenable or compliant. (obsolete) To graciously give; to vouchsafe. (obsolete) To reach a certain point; to settle on. (obsolete) To secretly make plans, usually to bring about a bad or illegal result; to conspire, to plot.
(archaic) On the day after tomorrow.
One of an array of open compartments for housing pigeons in a dovecote or pigeon loft.
(chiefly UK, Christianity) A home farm owned by a parish, assigned as part of a benefice to a parish priest, pastor, rector, or vicar to provide them with income.
To induct (someone) into a dignity or office with a formal ceremony.
(idiomatic) A desire or idea which is unlikely to materialize, or a plan which is unlikely to work; a near impossibility.

Preceded by the: a group of people characterized by being conservative or traditional; also, a method for accomplishing a task, a style, or a way of thinking that was employed in a former era, remembered either for its inferiority to the current method, or for its superiority over the new way.
(archaic) The deliberate slaughter of a people or a nation.

(typography, often programming) Especially in computer programming, and in the names of brands and organizations: a style of typography in which several words are concatenated together without any spaces between them, with the first letter of each word (sometimes excluding the first word) capitalized.
(obsolete) (A proper name for) an ape or monkey, especially a tame one kept for entertainment or as a pet.

(uncountable) Modification or regulation of something to achieve an appropriate measure or proportion; (countable) an instance of this. (art, uncountable) Variation (especially softening or toning down) of brightness, form, etc.; (countable) an instance of this. (biology, uncountable) Variation in the activity or form of a cell in response to changes in the environment; (countable) an instance of this. (engineering, physics, uncountable) Chiefly preceded by a descriptive word: modification of an electromagnetic wave or other oscillating carrier wave to apply a signal to it; (countable) an instance of this; also, the extent to which such a wave is modified; and the modified wave or signal. (music) (uncountable) Variation of the intensity, pitch, and tone of the sound of a musical instrument or voice; inflection; (countable) an instance of this. (uncountable) Changing of the key in a piece of music; also, the effect achieved by this as an element of harmony; (countable) an instance of this. (typography, countable) The contrast between the thick and thin parts of the strokes of a typeface.
(transitive) To book (“reserve in advance”) a large number of (hotel rooms, tickets, or other items) at one time.
(chiefly historical) Someone who handles and keeps bears, especially for use in public entertainments such as bearbaiting or dancing displays; a bearward.
Of a person: having a pleasing appearance; attractive; handsome.

(idiomatic, chiefly US, weaponry, historical) A nonfunctional imitation of a gun or an artillery piece, typically made of wood and usually intended to deceive enemy forces into overestimating one's available firepower.
Causing or involving calamity; disastrous.
In a manner regarded as factual or just; correctly, fairly, justly, truly; also, in a manner acceptable or excusable in the circumstances; understandably.

(chiefly Canada, US) A Native North American bisexual, homosexual, or gender-variant person; especially one belonging to a traditional tribal third-gender, fourth-gender, or transgender cultural category that has a ceremonial role.
(chemistry, dated) Conversion of a substance to, or reaction of a substance with, an arsenate (“a salt or ester of arsenic acid”).
(archaic) Of a person: crazy or insane, originally supposedly due to the influence of the Moon.
A person who likes or tends to express a contradicting viewpoint, especially from one held by a majority of people, usually because of nonconformity or spite.
(astronomy, archaic) Knowledge of the stars, especially the fixed stars; the branch of astronomy dealing with the fixed stars.

(Judaism) Either of two small leather cases containing scrolls with passages from the Torah, traditionally worn by a Jewish man (one on the arm (usually the left) and one on the forehead) and now sometimes by a woman at certain morning prayers as a reminder to obey the law as set out in the Bible; a tefilla. (figurative, chiefly derogatory) A profession of faith, or religious or traditional observance; also, a reminder. (archaic) A fringe which an Israelite was required to wear as a reminder to obey the law as set out in the Bible; (by extension) any fringe or border.
A friar of the religious order founded by Saint Francis of Assisi in 1209, now known as the Order of the Friars Minor.
(transitive, non-native speakers' English) To raise (something) to a better state; to elevate, to improve, to refine.
Of a group: closely connected or linked, as by a common bond, culture, or identity.
(countable, chiefly in the plural) singular of shenanigans: a deceitful confidence trick; also, an act of mischief; a prank, a trick; an act of mischievous play, especially by children.
A residential complex in a current or former communist country, chiefly one which was previously part of the Soviet Union; a microdistrict.
Preceded by the: A river in the Scottish Borders council area, Scotland, a tributary of Whiteadder Water; in full, Blackadder Water.
Someone who spreads fear.

(transitive) To set free (a person or group) from the oppression or restraint of another; to liberate. To cause (a place) to be free from the colonization or rule of another entity. (also reflexive) Often followed by from: chiefly with reference to slavery in the United States, and in Central and South America: to set free (oneself or someone) from imprisonment, or from serfdom or slavery. (chiefly law) To release (a minor) from the legal authority and custody which a parent or guardian has over them; also (Ancient Rome, historical), to release (a child) from the legal authority of the paterfamilias. (also reflexive, figurative) Often followed by from: to free (oneself or someone, or something) from some constraint or controlling influence (especially when evil or undue); also, to free (oneself or someone) from mental oppression. (obsolete) To place (something) under one's control; specifically (chiefly reflexive), to cause (oneself or someone) to become the slave of another person; to enslave; also, to subjugate (oneself or someone).
(botany, rare) The botanical study of ferns.
(historical) An officer on board a merchant ship who is in charge of the cargo and its sale; also, if there are two of such officers, the senior one, the other being the subcargo.
Of a thing: causing or having the nature of a scandal; regarded as so immoral or wrong as to be extremely disgraceful; despicable, shameful.
(derogatory, informal) Synonym of minor celebrity (“a celebrity of little importance or significance; someone famous for being famous”).
(neologism) A non-binary partner in an unmarried romantic relationship.

(uncountable, archaic) The action of spoliating, or forcibly seizing property; pillage, plunder; also, the state of having property forcibly seized; (countable) an instance of this; a robbery, a seizure.

Not in accord or harmony; conflicting, incompatible. Of people: disagreeing with each other; dissenting, quarrelsome. (also figurative) Of sounds: harsh, jarring; specifically (music), of musical notes or tunes: not in harmony; dissonant, inharmonious.
(US, rail transport, slang) The sound made by the exhaust of a steam locomotive.
(geology) A broad glacial valley formed during an ice age by meltwater flowing roughly parallel to the ice margin.
Regarding a person's action: in a handsome or good-looking manner.
(pathology, psychology) A condition where one does not possess a functioning "mind's eye" and cannot visualize imagery.

The quality or state of being uniform. (uncountable) The quality or state of having the same characteristics or form as other things, and lacking variety; (countable) an instance of this. (uncountable) Often followed by of: the quality or state of a thing always having the same characteristics or form; consistency or regularity in appearance or operation. (uncountable) Often followed by of: especially of one's life: lack of variety; tedious sameness; monotony. (uncountable, geology) The condition or state described by uniformitarianism (“the scientific principle that natural laws and processes operated in the past in the same way and at the same rates that they operate today, and sometimes in the same way everywhere in the universe as well”). (uncountable, especially religion) Adherence to or conformity with one viewpoint, set of observances, etc.
(mineralogy) A tetragonal-ditetragonal dipyramidal mineral containing arsenic, bismuth, calcium, copper, hydrogen, iron, and oxygen.
(rugby) A player who introduces the ball into a scrum.
A meeting or date.
(US, especially Southern US) An animal which is eaten by poor people during hard times, especially an armadillo or jackrabbit, but also a possum or squirrel.
To suggest (someone or something) in advance; to prefigure, to presage.

(historical) A mechanical lift for raising and lowering people through considerable distances; specifically (mining), a device by which miners ascend or descend in a shaft, consisting of a series of landings or sollars in the shaft and an equal number of shelves on a vertical rod which has an up and down motion equal to the distance between the successive landings. A person steps from a landing to a shelf and is raised or lowered to the next landing, upon which they then step, and so on, travelling by successive stages.
(originally US, neologism) Vicarious joy associated with seeing one's partner have a joyful romantic or sexual relationship with another person.
(psychology, sociology) Of a place, etc.: involving the arrangement of a group of people so that each person can see and interact with the others.

Synonym of adamant. Made of adamant (“an unspecified mineral or rock of virtually impenetrable hardness”). (figurative) Incapable of being broken, dissolved, or penetrated; impenetrable, unbreakable. Difficult to defeat or prevail over; unshakable, unyielding. Of a person: refusing to change one's mind; obstinate, stubborn. (obsolete) Having the quality of attracting or drawing; attractive, magnetic.

(uncountable) The state of being stagnant; (countable) an instance of this. (uncountable) The state of lacking flow or motion, usually causing a lack of freshness or health; (countable) an instance of this. (figurative, uncountable) The state of lacking activity, change or progress, or excitement in an unhealthy manner; inactivity, staleness; (countable) an instance of this. (economics, uncountable) The state of low or no growth in an economy; (countable) an instance of this.

One who challenges. One who confronts or opposes; a confronter, an opposer. (games, sports) One who plays against the current champion of a contest or game in hopes of winning and becoming the new champion. (chiefly law, obsolete) One who brings a legal claim; a claimant, a plaintiff; also, one who accuses; an accuser.
the study of heritable changes caused by the activation and deactivation of genes without any change in DNA sequence
To travel towards, to journey with a destination or goal in mind
Something, usually abnormal which grows out of something else
(medicine) an increase in the size of an organ due to swelling of the individual cells
(zoology): The study of ants
insipid and sentimental; spineless
very small, or petty
A biography of a saint
The perception of the position, weight and movement of the body, its limbs and muscles etc
To travel from place to place, especially on foot
Sleight of hand; "magic" trickery
One who studies and/or collects coins and currencies
The art and practice of rapid writing.
Marked by shame or disgrace; humiliating
To make fit for life in a house or home.
In a dexterous manner; skillfully, with precision, carefully
Factious, fragmenting, or tending to break into pieces.
To divide a geographic area into voting districts in such a way as to give an unfair advantage to one party in an election
Flowing like honey.
The condition of being poisoned as a result of a spider's bite
A conflicting difference or inconsistency between facts or sentiments
Something which or someone who belongs in the past and doesn't fit anymore in present days
Evil disposition or hatred towards another; the intention to harm others.
Having a surname that is the same as the place where one lives
Easily tired or wearied
To walk on ropes; to tightrope-walk
To to speak at length or be unnecessarily wordy, often in a patronizing or pompous manner.
Done or kept in secret, sometimes to conceal an illicit or improper purpose
To survey something (generally an enemy's land and position)
Mutually destructive, most often applied to warfare.
The working of miracles.
To cough up fluid from the lungs.
To make two or more events happen at exactly the same time, or at the same rate
To boast, brag or bluster pretentiously
Sad because of a recent event.
A sheltered, overly-academic existence or perspective, implying a disconnection or lack of awareness of reality or practical considerations
The use of the fingers and hands to communicate ideas, especially by the deaf
Of the next-to-last item in a sequence
To evade the truth; to waffle or be intentionally ambiguous.
To do something, particularly to perform or speak, without prior planning or thought; to act in an impromptu manner; to improvise
From that time on
To make gestures or motions while speaking.
The blundering use of an absurdly inappropriate word in place of a similar sounding one.
Apathetic, unconcerned, or nonchalant
To formally protest, object to, or rebel against
A large travelling case usually made of leather, and opening into two equal sections.
To remove something without anyone noticing
Bragging or boasting, especially in a false manner to another's detriment.
Of or related to twilight.
Noble and generous in spirit
The speculative biology of extraterrestrial life forms
In excess of what is required or sufficient
A book, list, or vocabulary of names, especially of people
Given to or marked by deliberate deceptiveness in behavior or speech
(architecture) A series of step-like projections at the top of a gable
Someone who quibbles over trivia, and raises petty, annoying objections.
A person who builds an aesthetically muscular physique, through diet and exercise, in order to compete in bodybuilding
A clever scheme or artful plot, usually crafted for evil purposes.
Chaos; tumultuous or lawless violence.
Done or kept in secret, sometimes to conceal an illicit or improper purpose
A fortunate occurrence created by unanticipated luck
(pathology) Difficulty in solving mathematical problems
