(originally US, idiomatic, informal) Chiefly preceded by on: a carefree lifestyle or situation, especially as resulting from being wealthy.
Entwined or linked together so as to form a network; interconnected, networked.
Synonym of beluga (“the beluga whale (Delphinapterus leucas), a cetacean found in the Arctic Ocean”)
A non-vegetarian who, for ethical reasons, does not eat meat from animals other than kangaroos, because they are sourced from the wild and not farmed.
Of food: delectable, delicious.
(uncountable, archaic) Behaviour adopted to demonstrate one's superiority; pretentious or snobbish behaviour; airs and graces.
(informal) Used as a dramatic pause, or to emphasize that something is frightening, thrilling, etc.
(originally and chiefly US, slang, humorous) An umbrella.

Senses relating to becoming involved with or entering into, especially entering into a contract. An act of incurring debt; also (generally), an act of acquiring something (generally negative). (archaic) An act of entering into a contract or agreement; specifically, a contract of marriage; a contracting; also (obsolete), a betrothal. (biology, medicine) The process of contracting or becoming infected with a disease.

(countable, nautical) Originally (now chiefly historical), a cask with a hole cut into its top, used to provide drinking water on board a ship; now (by extension, informal), a drinking fountain on a modern ship.
A group of people of mixed background, especially one with a common goal.
(countable) An angry or heated dispute.
(linguistics) Especially in Pacific linguistics: the lect (linguistic variety) spoken by a particular speech community.
(fantasy, science fiction) One who is not from the local world (whether a dimension, plane, planet, or universe); an alien.

(Ancient Greece, historical) A festival formerly held annually in Athens to honour the city's patron goddess Athena, involving animal sacrifices, a grand procession, and, every fourth year, athletic and musical contests.

(biotechnology) The planting and harvesting of vegetation that selectively concentrates specific metals from the environment into their tissues, for the purpose of commercial exploitation of the extracted metal.

(cooking, probably humorous) An ostrich stuffed with a turducken; that is, a boned chicken stuffed with some sort of breadcrumb or sausage stuffing, inside a boned duck, inside a boned turkey, inside a boned ostrich.
Inclined to make rapid decisions without due consideration; hasty, impulsive, rash.

(chiefly uncountable) The quality or state of being sovereign. Of a ruler (especially a monarch): supreme authority or dominion over something. (by extension) Of a nation or other polity: the state of being able to control resources, make laws independently, and otherwise govern itself without the coercion or concurrence of other polities. (by extension) Of a person: the liberty to decide one's actions and thoughts. Pre-eminent or superior excellence; also, superior ability to achieve something; mastery.
A holiday or leisure time spent drinking alcohol.
(archaic) One who is apt to find and frequent places where good food is available; especially one who comes uninvited to a meal; a parasite; a sponger.
(archaic) In a slighting manner; belittlingly, contemptuously.

Based on presumption or conjecture; inferred, likely, presumed. Often postpositive, as in heir presumptive: of an heir or heiress: presumed to be entitled to inherit unless someone with a superior entitlement is born. (embryology) Of a cell or tissue: which has yet to differentiate, but is presumed to develop into a particular body part.

(originally and chiefly US, scouting) An embroidered cloth patch awarded to a youth member of the Boy Scouts of America or other scouting organization who successfully completes an activity within an area of study (the badge bearing an image representing the activity), which is either sewn on to the sleeve of the scout's uniform or a sash worn with the uniform; hence, the award itself.

(loosely) Of a person: having a gender (identity) which is different from one's assigned sex; that is, the identity of a trans man, trans woman, or someone non-binary, for example, agender, bigender, or third-gender. (strictly) Of a person: having a gender (identity) which is opposite from the sex one was assigned at birth: being assigned male but having a female gender, or vice versa (that is, not including a non-binary identity).
(onomatopoeia) Used to represent the sound of a monkey's cry.
(music, slang) A person who habitually looks through crates of vinyl records at music shops, especially in pursuit of interesting or rare records.
A written description, often biographical.
Birdsong by a large number of birds occurring in the early morning.
(botany) Of a plant or its parts: bearing any substance (such as nectar or pollen) which is collected by bees to produce honey.
(idiomatic, originally nautical, dated) With willingness and zeal; with all one's heart or strength; earnestly, heartily.
A figurative place to which information is deliberately sent to be forgotten, or to which forgotten or lost information ends up; nowhere, oblivion.

(also attributive) Senses relating to something with a round shape, or which goes around. (originally nautical) A document (usually containing a complaint or petition) originally with the signatures arranged in a circle, and later often alphabetically, to disguise the order of signing and to indicate that the signatories are collectively responsible for it. A letter which is reproduced and sent to several people; specifically, one containing personal news sent at a particular time of year, often Christmas; a circular letter. A letter or piece of writing which is circulated among members of a group, to which each person makes a contribution before sending it to another person; also, a packet of letters circulated regularly in a fixed order among a group (often family members), whereby each person replaces their previous contribution with a new letter, and then sends the packet to the next recipient. A form of trading involving a packet of items which is circulated among members of a group, whereby each person take the items they want and replaces them with items of similar value, and then sends the packet to the next recipient. (figurative) A long, often tedious, list or piece of writing; a laundry list, a litany. (computing) A method of making use of several similar subsystems, assigning tasks to each of them in turn (for example, sending data to a destination down several communication links in turn to achieve greater combined speed). (gambling) A form of bet on the full set of possible combinations from a group of events, racehorses, teams, etc., such as the outcomes A and B, B and C, and A and C from a group consisting of A, B, and C. (music) The MIDI technique of using different sampled versions of the same sound for successive notes, to avoid an unnaturally repetitive effect. (online gaming) A method of dividing loot among a party of players by having the game assign loot, or an enemy corpse to loot, to each player in turn. (Devon, archaic) A small pancake. (originally US) A group activity in which the members take turns to perform an action. (originally US, sports, often attributive) The part of a tournament in which every player or team competes against each of the others in turn. (obsolete except historical) (Christianity, derogatory) The consecrated host used in the Eucharist. (clothing) A small ruff worn around the neck. (obsolete, road transport) A rim attached to the end of an axle of a horse carriage to prevent dirt from obstructing the axle's rotation; also, a loop from which a component of a horse carriage (such as a pole or spring) is suspended.
One who advocates liberty, either generally or in relation to a specific issue.
(Christianity, law, historical) The lay or temporal authority of a secular court to pronounce punishment (particularly capital punishment) of an offender tried by an ecclesiastical court.
(grammar, metaphysics (ontology), also attributive) A term, word, etc., that describes a plurality greater than another plural.
(economics) Prolonged high inflation accompanied by stagnant growth, often with recession and high unemployment.
Of, pertaining to, or resembling, a tempest; also, of a place: frequently experiencing tempests; (very) stormy.
A list of tasks arising during a meeting that are put aside to be dealt with later.
(rail transport, road transport) A vehicle that generates smoke; specifically, an automobile or a train.

(transitive) To cause (a sound) to be (repeatedly) bounced against one or more surfaces; to re-echo. Followed by on (to): to deflect or divert (flames, heat, etc.) on to something. (chemistry, metallurgy, archaic) To heat (something) by deflecting flames on to, or passing flames over, it. (chiefly sciences) To repeatedly reflect (heat, light, or other radiation). (obsolete) To drive, force, or push (someone or something) back; to repel, to repulse. To send (something) back from where it came. Of light or sound: to fall on or hit (a surface or other thing); also, to fill or spread throughout (a space or other thing). (rare) To beat or hit (something) repeatedly.
A woman who is high-ranking, socially prominent, or has a dignified character, especially one who is advanced in age and haughty.
(idiomatic) An excess of something; a large yield.
(informal) Used to indicate the position of a number which is uncertain or unimportant.

(transitive) (business) To artificially increase (the price of a commodity; originally crude oil, especially in a single shipment) by a group of dealers buying and selling it among themselves before it is sold to a party outside the group. (chiefly computing, electronics) To connect (several computer devices or peripherals, or other components) in sequence with each other, usually such that the output of one component forms the input of another; also (followed by to), to connect (a computer device or peripheral, or other component) to another component in such a sequence.
(dated) A fixture, usually hanging from a ceiling, for holding electric lamps; specifically, a chandelier lit by electricity rather than with candles or gas.
According to the required or usual ceremonies, formalities, or rituals; specifically (Christianity, obsolete), to ceremonial laws in the Bible.
Suggestive of Jay Gatsby, the titular character of the novel The Great Gatsby (1925): enigmatic; extravagant; nouveau riche, etc.
(historical) Synonym of Paschal term (“the fourteenth day of the first lunar month of spring, formerly used in calculating dates”).
(idiomatic, intransitive, chiefly in the negative) To restrain oneself when speaking by withholding some comments or using euphemisms in order to be polite, tactful, etc.
An instance of some phenomenon in the sky said to signal bad weather, such as an incomplete or secondary rainbow, or a parhelion or sun dog; a weather-gall or water-gall.
(UK, often politics, dated, derogatory, figurative) Of local interest or significance only; petty, parochial.
Chiefly in terraqueous globe (that is, the Earth): consisting of both land and water.
(UK, informal) Any ordinary woman, especially an older woman.

Lasting or enduring forever; endless, eternal. Chiefly of a deity or other supernatural being: having always existed and will continue to exist forever; eternal. (philosophy) Synonym of sempiternal (“having infinite temporal duration, rather than outside time and thus lacking temporal duration altogether”).
(idiomatic) Certainly not; definitely not; not at all.
Especially of a person. (in a positive sense, sometimes humorous) Eliciting respect; awe-inspiring, imposing. (in a negative sense) Eliciting dread or fear; appalling, formidable.

(military) Of a nuclear weapons strategy: encouraging deterrence by guaranteeing an automatic, immediate, and overwhelming response to an attack, even if the command-and-control infrastructure has been damaged by the enemy's first strike.
Of, pertaining to, or from Singapore, or its language or people.
Of an animal: having feet which are naturally flat; (specifically) of a horse: having hoofs with soles close to the ground.
(Internet slang) A nail polish aficionado.
(uncommon) Nostalgia for something one has not experienced or which never existed.
(LGBTQ) Not having a fixed gender; having a gender which changes from time to time.
(Internet, uncountable) A form of Internet-based computer animation characterized by unpredictable montages of pop culture images set to music; (countable) an instance of this.
(historical, usually in the plural) A coin placed on the eyelid of a deceased person to keep the eye closed.

A dog which serves people by helping them with activities of daily living, with search and rescue, or with other (non-draft and non-farming) tasks. (law enforcement, military) A dog which assists the work of military or law enforcement officers. (chiefly Canada, US) A dog which assists a person who has a physical disability or a mental disability such as post-traumatic stress disorder.

(countable, uncountable, often in the plural) Emotions or feelings arising from or relating to aesthetic or moral standards, especially those which are sensitive and thus likely to be hurt or offended.
A door leading to a cellar.

(Ancient Rome, government, historical) A council of three magistrates ruling jointly; specifically, the First Triumvirate (60 or 59 – 53 B.C.E.) or the Second Triumvirate (43 – 33 or 27 B.C.E.); the office of a triumvir (“one of such magistrates”), or of the three triumviri.
The act or process of unifying.

(biology, geography) An isthmus or other land connection between landmasses, chiefly one which existed during prehistoric times, by which it is theorized that animals and plants were able to spread from one landmass to another.
A period of time between the end of one monarch's reign and the accession of their successor.
In a dissatisfied, frustrated, or upset mood; in a bad temper or ill humour.
Synonym of pathfinder. One who discovers or makes a new path or way through an untraversed region. (figurative) One who is the first to do something in a particular field; an innovator, a pioneer.

(transitive) (medicine, specifically oncology) Of a disease (especially cancer) or a tumour: to form a metastasis (“secondary focus away from the primary site”) in (a body organ). (chiefly US, figurative) To disseminate or spread (something, often an undesirable thing), especially in a destructive manner.
(chemistry) A radioactive metallic transuranic chemical element (symbol Md) with atomic number 101, which is artificially produced in a particle accelerator.

(chiefly logic, philosophy) Of or relating to existence. Concerning the very existence of something, especially with regard to evading extinction. Of an assertion, etc.: assuming or suggesting the existence of something. Of or relating to existentialism (“a philosophical movement emphasizing the uniqueness of each human existence in freely making its self-defining choices”).
(US, humorous) A supposed snack offered to a person as a prank; if they accept, they are punched or otherwise hurt in some way.

(uncountable) The action of forbearing (“abstaining or refraining from doing something”); (countable) an instance of this. (uncountable) The action of refraining from enforcing something which is due (such as a debt or obligation) or a right which one has; (countable) an instance of this. (uncountable) Restraint and tolerance under provocation; patient self-control; indulgence, lenity.
(idiomatic, informal) A short sleep, especially when taken during the day; a nap.
The purchasing of objects as a form of mental relaxation
(Australia) (colloquial) Imaginary incapacitated person
To throw out of a window
Relating to charity, alms, or almsgiving; intended for the distribution of charity; as, an eleemosynary corporation
The property of a word of sounding like what it represents
Stubbornly unwilling to compromise or moderate a position; unreasonable
After a meal, especially after dinner
Frighteningly monstrous and otherworldly
To leave quickly or in a hurry; to take oneself off; to depart
A collection of written passages, used to learn an unfamiliar language

A legendary knot which carried a prophecy that whoever loosed the knot would become ruler of all Asia. Alexander the Great solved the puzzle by slicing through the knot and took it as a sign of Zeus's favor.
The study or theory of the methodical interpretation of text, especially holy texts
Impudent; open and without shame
Going off in all directions at once
(of a place) Composed of people from all over the world.
Technical or scientific language used to convey a false impression of meaningful technical or scientific content
A figure of speech where an expected word in an idiom is replaced unexpectedly by an unusual one
To completely alter the form of something
Causing tortuous pain or anguish.
Attended by, or making, a loud and tumultuous noise.
Of or related to the interpretation of dreams
Stubborn, cranky, or surly
Arrogantly superior; showing contemptuous indifference; haughty
A word in a foreign language bearing a deceptive resemblance to a word in one's own language
In a careful manner, with scruple; done with careful attention to detail
In a lying or deceitful manner
(logic) An invalid argument, the conclusion of which cannot be logically deduced from the premises.
Of or pertaining to an ecclesiastic or priestly order.
Of, or relating to prostitutes or prostitution.
A cover for the back or arms of a chair or sofa, originally intended to prevent them from being soiled by macassar oil
Of or relating to astronomy.
Condition of favoring or liking; tendency towards; proclivity
Of, relating to, or being self-taught
Giving off bubbles; fizzy.
As a result or consequence of something
Marked by harsh spoken or written abuse; invective; scolding; abusive
Marked by abstinence or restraint, especially in relation to food or drink
With a loud, resonant sound.
(geometry) A polyhedron with twelve faces; the regular dodecahedron has twelve regular pentagons as faces
(idiomatic) In the event; should there be a need
Contemptuous of authority; willfully disobedient; rebellious
That causes or induces sneezing
Amorous, lustful; feeling sexy
Premeditated; planned ahead of time
Following, afterwards in either time or place
Not noble; unbecoming true honor or dignity; mean.
Of, relating to, or resulting from the retention of juvenile characteristics by an adult
That eats of dead or decaying animal flesh
(obsolete) Totally worthless object or person; something that is just in the way
To draw a line around; to encircle.
As a result or consequence of something
A period of one day and one night; a date
A geometric curve traced by a fixed point on the radius line outside one circle which rotates inside the perimeter of another circle
With excessive vanity or unwarranted pride
To evade, to equivocate using subterfuge; to deliberately obfuscate.
A mythical Christmas character made by decorating a short log. It is "fed" each day from December 8th until Christmas, at which time it is sung to and beaten with sticks until it "poops" out gifts
One (especially a child) who is very excited or enthusiastic to begin a task
Meeting end-to-end or at the ends.
Excessive; exaggerated; outrageous
A ghostly double of a living person, especially one that haunts such a person.
(law) This for that; giving something to receive something else.
A joint or the collection of joints at which something is articulated, or hinged, for bending.
A behavior or way of thinking that is characteristic of a person.
Freely; in the manner of a gift, without being earned.
With heavy breathing, as if snoring; in a stertorous manner
The act of exclaiming; violent outcry; vehement utterance of the voice
Rudely contemptuous; showing contumely; insolent or disdainful
An earnest affirmation or declaration of support
Of or relating to the other side of the mountains
Expressed in more words than are necessary.
A consequence or ensuing result of some action
Emitting light as a result of being heated.
Hating or disliking mankind
A person who spends a lot of time sitting or lying down, often watching television, eating snacks, and drinking alcohol
(historical) The lamb eaten at Passover.
Unhappy or unfortunate.
A pattern along the top of a parapet (fortified wall), most often in the form of multiple, regular, rectangular spaces in the top of the wall, through which arrows or other weaponry may be shot
(heraldry) A diminutive of the chevron, always borne in closely placed pairs
Person or entity whose name one does not want to mention but which is known to the person to whom one is speaking
The art and technology of fireworks and related military applications.
Of or pertaining to the making of maps
From an external source; not innate or inherent, foreign.
To undertake a dangerous action in the service of a group
With heavy breathing, as if snoring; in a stertorous manner
The act of exclaiming; violent outcry; vehement utterance of the voice
Rudely contemptuous; showing contumely; insolent or disdainful
(psychoanalysis) The first time a child sees its parents copulating
(Ireland) Cynical self-interest
Pertaining to the world before the Fall of man; innocent, unspoiled
That provides recompense; compensatory, rewarding
That cannot be procured; unobtainable
A public passage, path or road affording communication between two places; a main street or highway
Pertaining to or situated in an interstice; located or happening in an intervening space
Stubbornly unwilling to obey authority
A drug for the treatment of intestinal worm infestation
An overnight stay; action (or instance) of abiding through the night at a location.
Physically resembling a labyrinth; with the qualities of a maze.
Wearing spectacles (glasses)
(Buddhism, Chinese folk religion) A type of being condemned to desire more than it can consume
Pertaining to a nickname, usually indicating intimacy with the person
(idiomatic, plurale tantum) Blindness to criticism or objectivity directed at a person that one is in love with
Having a big belly.
A person who, by virtue of sovereignty, holds the exclusive right to create and confer legitimate titles of nobility and orders of chivalry
A person who collects match-related items, like matchbox labels, matchboxes, matchbooks, or matchbook covers
(medicine) Having a normal body temperature
(of multiple years or of a date range) Taking place a specifed number of years following the assumed birth of Jesus Christ

One who is unhappy, or extols being miserable as a virtue; a philosopher of pessimism
Cheerless, dreary.
Something given in exchange for goods or services rendered.
(medicine) Any adverse effect (or complication) resulting from medical treatment
Extravagant or flowery writing, especially in a literary work
An essential or indispensable element, condition, or ingredient
Containing or tinged with blood
A decorative cake distributed among friends or visitors on the festival of Twelfth Night (which is either the evening of January 5th or of January 6th, depending on interpretation)
(geology, of a map) Showing the previous location of geological features, correcting for any intervening crustal movements
The act of gathering blueberries
A substance that induces lactation
A kind of technobabble particular to the Star Trek universe, with a particularly heavy emphasis on configurations of pseudoscientific particles and waves
(idiomatic) To make a choice with an uncertain outcome; to take a chance

The moving of cattle or other grazing animals to new pastures, often quite distant, according to the change in season
A decorative cake distributed among friends or visitors on the festival of Twelfth Night (which is either the evening of January 5th or of January 6th, depending on interpretation)
(geology, of a map) Showing the previous location of geological features, correcting for any intervening crustal movements
The act of gathering blueberries
Filled with passion, impassioned: young ministers, deeply impressed and longing to pour out the burning, impassionate zeal of their own souls, are apt to abuse the use of this figure (George Hott).
A trained clinician who operates the heart-lung machine during cardiac and other surgeries.
The property of being, or that which causes something to be, resplendent.
(idiomatic) To walk a regular route.
(botany) A hybrid which is formed by direct hybridization of two species, not other hybrids.
The condition or state of being predominant; ascendancy, domination, preeminence, preponderance.
(archaic, now only humorous or regional) Food, provender.
(slang, fiction) The breakdown of society as a result of a zombie outbreak.
(US) A situation where the welfare system keeps people on social insurance because entering low-paid work does not result into a significant income increase.
Having three components or parts.
Exhibiting parsimony; sparing in expenditure of money; frugal to excess; penurious; niggardly; stingy.
(often science fiction) In a rotating reference frame, against the direction of spin.
(US) A room found in homes in Arizona, typically a patio that has been covered and screened so as to create an outdoor feeling while avoiding excessive heat and insects.
A tourist who takes part in voluntourism.
The act of vituperating; severely blaming or censuring.
A medicine which preserves or defends against disease; a preventive.
Endlessly; for ever; neverendingly.
Defective and impossible to materially correct or set aright.
Prevented by bad weather from doing something, such as travelling.
(informal) The practice of men splaying their legs open wide when sitting on public transport, thus occupying more than one seat.
Someone or something, especially a sports team, that defeats a larger and superior opponent.
To take out of service or to render unusable.
(informal) An actress who appears in many horror films.
(idiomatic) A signature.
(by extension of the proper noun) A woman of extraordinary powers; a superwoman.

Basic, old-fashioned or traditional; specifically (journalism) shoe-leather journalism or shoe-leather reporting: journalism involving walking from place to place observing things and speaking to people, rather than sitting indoors at a desk.
(chiefly uncountable) Benevolent altruism with the intention of increasing the well-being of mankind.
(idiomatic) Abstaining from drinking any alcoholic drink, usually in the sense of having given it up (as opposed to never having partaken); teetotal.
(Christianity, historical) An open-air religious service held by Calvinists in the Low Countries during the Reformation, typically in rural areas beyond the reach of civic authorities.
(chiefly law) The condition, or an act, of doing wrong; the act of committing a tort.
(chiefly British) A building or place housing shops or stalls where all sorts of (especially exotic) manufactured articles are collected for sale.
Reluctantly, unwillingly.
Government under the control of a nation's worst or least-qualified citizens.
A tropical hardwood tree, Manilkara bidentata.
A dessert consisting of ice cream encased in cake and meringue and briefly baked.
(rare) A mental condition of thinking that one has become a cat, which is usually manifested in the adoption of feline mannerisms and habits.
(idiomatic, journalism) A period, usually during the summertime, when news media tend to place increased emphasis on reporting light-hearted, offbeat, or bizarre stories.
(economics) The attempt to profit by manipulating the economic or political environment, for example, by seeking subsidies.
(ophthalmology) An instrument used to test for color blindness by measuring quantitative and qualitative anomalies in color perception.
The phenomenon occurring when the Sun does not set but only approaches the horizon at midnight; it occurs near the summer solstice in the polar regions.
(US, idiomatic, humorous) A catch phrase for shifting attention away from a problem or serious social issue by humorously laying responsibility on Canada.
(idiomatic, often followed by for) To make future development easier.
An anthelmintic medication used to treat a number of types of parasitic worm infections, including clonorchiasis, cysticercosis, opisthorchiasis, schistosomiasis, and tapeworm infections.
(idiomatic) To learn, or attempt to learn, what kind of person one is and what one wants in life.
(idiomatic, also attributive) A journalist or pundit, especially one on television, who presents or discusses issues of the day.
(chiefly US) An event, particularly on radio or television, showcasing the talents of amateur performers.
A branch of an olive tree offered as an emblem of peace.
(geology) The proposed current geological epoch, in which human activities have a powerful effect on the global environment.
(Trinidad and Tobago) A traditional character in the Trinidad and Tobago Carnival dressed as a devil, mostly naked and covered in molasses or grease and a colourful dye.
At once, at the same moment in time.
(slang, humorous) The time of the first beer (or alcoholic beverage) of the day.

(Christianity (Christology)) The state of the human nature of Jesus Christ being entirely dependent on, and not existing independently of, the divine nature of God as a whole (which is the hypostasis of the Holy Trinity comprising God the Father, God the Son, and God the Holy Spirit), or individual persons of the Trinity such as the Father and the Holy Spirit.
A group of artists who worked in the studio of, or under the influence of, Leonardo da Vinci.
Unbounded by rules or conventions; unrestrained.
A current of water which flows under the surface, and often in a different direction from surface currents.
(Exmoor) Synonym of bad-tempered.
(Eastern Orthodoxy) A ritual sprinkler used in some Christian rites.
One who describes a utopia.
(transitive) To recommend the opposite or negation of; to advise against.
(idiomatic) A very small gift or donation which, however, represents a great sacrifice on the part of the giver.
Thirsty for blood: inexorably violent or eager for bloodshed; murderous.
Having one's head in the clouds: prone to daydreaming, or thinking impractically.
Clustered together into a mass.
(nonstandard, proscribed, sometimes humorous) Irrespective, regardless.
(darts) A woman who escorts a player to the stage at a darts event.
A thick soup made of smoked finnan haddock, milk, onions, and potatoes, a local speciality of Cullen in Moray, Scotland.
Contemplation of one's navel as an aid to meditation.
A binder clip with rigid handles.
(idiomatic, often attributively) A person who, or thing which, is in a gradually worsening situation without any realization of the peril until it is too late.
(rare) Exalting or supporting conflict or war.
(idiomatic) A thing which has good and bad parts, but is overall spoilt by the bad.
(neurology, psychology) A neurological or psychological phenomenon whereby a particular sensory stimulus triggers a second kind of sensation.
(obstetrics, veterinary medicine) A woman or female animal who is pregnant for the first time, or who has been pregnant once.
(zoology) Any animal that cares for its offspring, either eggs or juveniles, by holding them in its mouth for extended periods of time.
(idiomatic) The majority; a large or generous portion.
(physiology) Pertaining to steatopygia; having fat or prominent buttocks.
Comprising things that are wholly from the United States of America; completely made in the United States.
(formerly obsolete, rare, now humorous) A coward.
(physics) A wave that can be modulated, either in amplitude, frequency, or phase, to carry or transmit images, music, speech, or other signals.
(humorous) An intuitive feeling, usually of something being dangerous or risky; (more generally) instinct, intuition.
(archaic) A methodical inquiry or investigation.
(law, postpositive) Of a legal obligation: arising from a contractual relationship.
A person who is believed to ward off witchcraft and heal through magical powers; a shaman.

(slang, humorous, derogatory) A person who acts (or may supposedly act) obsessively or even dangerously towards another person that the first person was previously, or wishes to be, in a relationship with.
(uncountable) Business (especially big business) connected to agriculture, either owning or operating large-scale farms, or catering to those who do.
(idiomatic) (originally) by the authority or power of; (now), because of; on the grounds of; by reason of.
A feeling or sense of celebration or enjoyment.
Synonym of Christkind (“a personification of the baby Jesus who, in German-speaking parts of Europe, takes the place of Santa Claus in bringing gifts to people at Christmastime”).
(idiomatic) Synonym of Midas touch (“the ability to achieve financial reward (or, more generally, success) easily and consistently”)
(rare) Pertaining to one's mother tongue.
The common weasel, little weasel, or simply weasel (Mustela nivalis), the smallest member of the weasel genus Mustela, which is native to Eurasia, North America, and North Africa.
(humorous) A metaphorical piece of headgear supposedly worn by a person to assist them in thinking about how to solve a problem.

(patent law, informal, derogatory) A company, person, etc., that owns and enforces patents in an aggressive and opportunistic manner, often with no intention of marketing or promoting the subjects of the patents.

A stone, either naturally exposed or intentionally embedded in a river during a drought, which is dated and often inscribed to mark the water level as a warning to future generations that they may face famine if the water reaches that low level again. Such stones are common in Central Europe.
To accompany the driver of a vehicle on a journey as an armed escort (originally with a shotgun); (by extension) to accompany someone in order to assist and protect.

(informal) Causing great awe or surprise.

(originally US) A person who rubbernecks; someone who cranes their neck as though it were made of rubber to see something (such as a tourist attraction) or to watch an event (such as an accident); a rubberneck.

(US, politics, historical) In the United States in the 1960s (chiefly 1961), any one of a number of trips taken by bus or other forms of transport through parts of the southern U.S., made by groups of civil rights activists demonstrating their opposition to racial prejudice and segregation.
Lucky and pleasant but with some problems.
Maintaining a stiff upper lip.
Tending to discomfit (“embarrass greatly; upset the composure of, disconcert”).
(chiefly US, informal) Boisterous, energetic, noisy, and difficult to control.
The art or craft of compiling, writing, and editing dictionaries.
(humorous, informal) An imaginary device that measures cringeworthiness (the extent to which something makes one cringe; that is, flinch or recoil with embarrassment).
Concerned with people's welfare, and the alleviation of suffering; compassionate, humane.

Incapable of being cleared of clouds.

(zoology, chiefly entomology) The situation where interspecific (interspecies) mating fails to produce hybrids and thus reduces the fitness of the species involved.

(Christianity, philosophy) An attempt, or an argument attempting, to justify that human beings are fundamentally good despite the commission of evil acts by some people.
(formal, rare or obsolete) A question which cannot properly be given a one-word answer like “yes” or “no”.

A curtailment or reduction. (specifically) An act of reducing expenses; economizing. (specifically) An act of terminating the employment of a worker or making an employee redundant, often to reduce expenses; a layoff.
(British, informal) A job or task that is easy to do; a position that requires little work or is undemanding; a sinecure.
Of, related to, or typifying extortion (“the practice of obtaining money or other property by the use of force or threats”).
A tough bag, usually cylindrical, filled with air, sand, or other material, often suspended so that it swings freely, which is punched or kicked when practising sports such as boxing or martial arts.

(botany) A plant which was introduced to an area by humans (or arrived naturally, but from an area in which it was present as a human introduction) and became naturalized before 1500 C.E. (but especially in prehistoric times).

Which cannot be refuted; clearly right, incontrovertible, indisputable, irrefutable.

(uncountable) The action of part or all of a national population violently rising up against the government or other authority; (countable) an instance of this; a revolt, an uprising; specifically, one that is at an initial stage or limited in nature.

(humorous, often attributively) A person overly concerned with ensuring that a wedding goes exactly as they envision it.
(uncountable) Associating, or coming together in a union; (countable) an instance of this.

(idiomatic, chiefly Canada, US) The practice of trying to stop problems, etc., that repeatedly occur in an apparently random manner; also, the act of dealing with such matters in a piecemeal manner without achieving a complete solution.

A Jewish pâté-like food made by mincing beef or chicken liver and onions which have been broiled or fried in schmaltz (“chicken fat”) together with hard-boiled eggs; it is usually spread on to bread.

(epidemiology) The protection given to part of the surviving community against an epidemic of a contagious disease when a significant number of the population are immunized or develop immunity if they survive from the disease.
(singular) The world, a place where difficulties and the sorrows of life are felt, especially (Christianity) as a place to be left behind when one dies and goes to heaven.

(informal) A romantic novel, usually in a historical setting, with frank depictions of sexual activity, especially one in which the female protagonist is seduced; (by extension) a film, television programme, etc., featuring such activity.

The process of coming to resemble the US state of Florida in some respect.
(politics) An election that the ruler or political party which is in power calls before the regularly scheduled election time.
(typography) The punctuation mark "?", used at the end of a sentence to indicate a question.

(architecture) Of a building: constructed using a load-bearing timber frame with the spaces (panels) between the timbers filled with bricks, stone, or wattle and daub, etc. (the infill), especially if the timber frame is visible on the outside of the building.
(uncountable) The activity of applying pigments to the interior surfaces of caves to create images, especially when carried out in prehistoric times.
(meteorology) Of, relating to, or occurring the stratosphere (“the region of the uppermost atmosphere”).
With great attention to detail; complete, thorough.
Existence together with the bodily faculties.
In a plethoric manner: excessively, overabundantly.
(colloquial, hyperbolic) A large amount of money, especially one paid for something.
(mathematics) A logarithmic line on a Gunter's scale, which is used to perform the multiplication and division of numbers mechanically with only dividers.

(nautical) Of a boat or ship: having the hull constructed using planks or plates laid so that each plank overlaps the edge of the plank or plate below it, and in the case of plates, behind it; the planks were traditionally secured by nails which had been clenched (or clinched), that is, after being hammered through the planks the points of the nails were bent to make them unremovable.
(obsolete) Greater physical weight. (specifically, weaponry, historical) The excess of weight of that part of a cannon behind the trunnions over that in front of them.

The shrub Bixa orellana, which is native to Mexico and northern South America; the arils covering its seeds are a source of the orange-red colourant annatto, and the ground seeds are used in traditional Caribbean, Central American, and South American cuisine.
(uncountable) Traditional medical practices developed and used by non-physicians, typically involving customary homespun techniques and medicinal remedies derived from native plants.
A small fork usually with two or three tines, used for eating dishes served as appetizers (such as fruit cocktails and shrimp cocktails), and for picking up olives and other small snacks.
(idiomatic, often literary or poetic) Bread or some other staple foodstuff.
(originally US, informal) An ordinary person who works in a non-management position, especially one who works for wages rather than a salary.

An activity, process, or state that is the result of another; a by-product, a consequence.

(epidemiology) A person infected with a pathogen who is responsible for spreading it to many other people. (also attributively) An event or place which leads to the spread of an infectious disease to many people.
The simple material pleasures of life.

(also attributively) Often preceded by in the. The state of not being owned by anyone, and thus freely usable by everyone. The state of not or no longer being confidential or secret.
(British, informal) Often used as a response to some (anticipated) success: lovely; fantastic, great.
(British, dated) An exclamation of astonishment, confusion, despair, etc.: my sainted aunt, my sainted uncle.

A prince who rules a country as a regent in place of a monarch who is unfit to rule for whatever reason.

Someone who tends to drop things; (more generally) someone who is clumsy or uncoordinated; a klutz.
An inner sense which distinguishes what is right from what is wrong, functioning as a guide for morally appropriate behaviour.

(also attributively) gerund of merchandise (archaic) Trade in merchandise. (originally US) The promotion of goods for sale in a store, especially through advertising, attractive displays, discounts, etc.; also (generally), the promotion of any goods or services for sale. (specifically) The promotion of a film, music group, theatre production, etc., through the sale of goods bearing motifs associated with the subject being promoted; also, such goods themselves collectively; merchandise.
Especially in the Tour de France: the yellow shirt worn by the leader of a stage of a cycling race, which is then awarded to the cyclist who wins the race with the fastest overall time.
A series of two events that causes adverse effects; a twofold blow or setback.
(countable) A particular thing. A distinctive characteristic or quality; a peculiarity. (obsolete) A particular case or matter.
(intransitive, idiomatic) Often followed by between or to: to establish friendly relations or links.

In full normal backwardation: the situation in a futures market where the price for future delivery of a commodity (the forward price) is lower than the price for immediate delivery (the spot price) or nearer delivery, generally arising from a near-term shortage of the commodity.
(physiology) The perception or sense of time.
One (especially an older woman) who behaves as, is regarded as equivalent to, or represents a mother for another person or group of people.
(originally US) Women's casual trousers, usually fairly form-fitting, that end at the calves.
Of a plant: having flowers that appear earlier than the leaves.
A gesture of greeting among Star Trek fans, consisting of a raised hand with the palm forward and the thumb extended, and the fingers parted between the middle finger and ring finger.

(astronomy) An arbitrary formation of stars perceived as a figure (especially one from mythology) or pattern, or a division of the sky including it, especially one officially recognized by astronomers; an asterism. A mountain in the Himalayas, on the border of Solukhumbu district, Koshi, Nepal and Tingri County, Shigatse, Tibet Autonomous Region, China; the world's highest mountain above sea level.

(nautical) The navigation of waters free from hazards or unfavourable winds. Obsolete spelling of plane sailing (“a technique for navigation using the assumption that the journey occurs over a plane or flat surface rather than the actual curved surface of the Earth, which is sufficiently accurate over short distances”).

(electronics) Being or relating to a method of manually constructing electronic circuits prior to automation and printed circuit boards, where components were individually mounted on the chassis prior to soldering. (telecommunications) Employing a simple network topology with a dedicated link between two endpoints. (transport) Travelling directly from source to destination without passing through a hub.
Synonym of comfort (“contentment, ease”).

(chiefly US) The national holiday of Independence Day in the United States, celebrated on the fourth day in July to mark the anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence from Great Britain in 1776.

Any gas (such as carbon dioxide, a chlorofluorocarbon (CFC), or methane) that contributes to the greenhouse effect when released into the atmosphere by absorbing infrared radiation emitted by a planet's surface, thus increasing the surface temperature.
